text: The implemented topological_ordering function fails all tests because it does not properly account for all dependencies between nodes. It starts from nodes with no incoming edges but incorrectly appends nodes only when their outgoing nodes are already in the ordered list, missing nodes that should appear earlier to satisfy dependencies. This results in incorrect node ordering and missing nodes in the output.

data.label: topological_ordering produces incorrect node ordering and misses nodes in final output
Tests show actual output does not match expected order and output is incomplete
Function only adds nextnode when all of its outgoing_nodes are already in ordered_nodes
Proper topological ordering requires placing each node before any nodes it points to
